free_text: The evaporation of alpha-particles from heavy nuclei
The Berkeley 2.2 m variable energy
cyclotron was used in experiment.
Detectors were a pair of dE-E
telescopes mounted 20deg apart on a rotatable arm.
The dE counters were 75 and 125 mu-m
phosphorus-diffused silicon transmission detectors,
and the E-counters were 3000 mu-m lithium- drifted
silicon detectors.
No information on uncertainties are given.
Compiled by S.H.
Data taken from fig.2 of the reference
Metallic Ta foil with thickness of 3-4 mg/cm2
Corrections were made for
-  electronic dead-time
-  target thickness and orientation
-  counter efficiency
